title: corylus cornuta subsp. cornuta
intro: Corylus cornuta subsp. cornuta, also known as American hazelnut or eastern hazelnut, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its edible nuts, heart-shaped leaves, and brown or gray bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. The health benefits of C. cornuta subsp. cornuta include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil infusion, and its nuts can be consumed raw or roasted, offering a rich source of protein, fiber, and essential nutrients.
